details: :sp stacks a view above/below
Ctrl-w s does the same from Normal
Both share the buffer; edits sync

tips.summary: Open a file in a split :split {file}
details: Relative paths start at the project root
~/ or an absolute path works too
A bare filename is found anywhere in the project
:vs opens a side-by-side view
Ctrl-w v does the same from Normal
Great for comparing two spots

tips.summary: Open a file in a vsplit :vsplit {file}
details: Relative paths start at the project root
~/ or an absolute path works too
A bare filename is found anywhere in the project
Ctrl-w c or :close shuts this split
The file stays open; only the view closes
:only keeps the current split
Ctrl-w o does the same from Normal mode

tips.summary: Cycle through splits Ctrl-w w / W
details: Ctrl-w w goes to the next split
Ctrl-w W goes to the previous split

tips.summary: Move to a split Ctrl-w h/j/k/l
details: Ctrl-w then a direction key moves you
h left, l right, j down, k up
Map Ctrl-h/j/k/l to skip the Ctrl-w

tips.summary: Make a split taller or shorter
details: Ctrl-w + grows it, Ctrl-w - shrinks it
Add a count for bigger steps: 10 Ctrl-w +

tips.summary: Make a split wider or narrower
details: Ctrl-w > grows it, Ctrl-w < shrinks it
Add a count for bigger steps: 10 Ctrl-w >
Ctrl-w = gives every split equal size
Handy to reset after manual resizing
gt goes to the next tab
gT goes to the previous tab

tips.summary: Jump to a tab by number {count}gt
details: 2gt jumps to tab 2, 5gt jumps to tab 5
Useful when you have several tabs open
:tabclose closes the active tab page
Use it when you want to keep the rest
:tabonly closes all other tab pages
Use it to reduce a crowded tab layout
:tabmove 0 moves the tab to the start
:tabmove with no count moves it to the end

tips.summary: Maximize a split's height or width
details: Ctrl-w _ gives this split max height
Ctrl-w | gives it max width
